title: Auto-memory index snapshot — 2026-05-05
topic: Snapshot of MEMORY.md (auto-memory index for the Vector project) at the close of the PLA-0017 planning session, with one-line entries for every persisted memory file
summary: Frozen view of the auto-memory index at end-of-session — every memory file currently persisted, with its hook line and intent.
content:

  1. What this is
  2. How to read this

What this is

This is a frozen copy of the auto-memory index (MEMORY.md) for the Vector project at the close of the PLA-0017 planning session. The live index is loaded into Claude's context every conversation; this snapshot exists so the project itself has a checkpointed record of what was persisted, in case the live index drifts or gets pruned.

The index file format is one bullet per memory file: - [Title](file.md) — one-line hook. Each linked file lives in ~/.claude/projects/-Users-rick-Documents-MMFFDev-Projects-MMFFDev---Vector/memory/ and contains the full memory body with frontmatter.

How to read this

Each entry below pairs the memory file name with its hook line (the same one-line description the live index carries). Memory type is implied by the filename prefix:

  • boot*.md — session-restore snapshots
  • test_account*.md / test_accounts_*.md — fixture credentials
  • feedback_*.md — durable user feedback / hard rules
  • css_*.md — CSS-convention notes
  • project_*.md — long-running project state pickups

Session restores

  • boot1.md — Session restore — 2026-05-01 instance 1: Launcher reorg sync from MBP17, xcodegen build pipeline, then untrack build artifacts; branch main clean at 1492398, story counter 00223.
  • boot3.md — Session restore — 2026-05-05 instance 3: PLA-0013 dev-UI migration done on main (18/18 AC); integration branch redundant, awaiting cleanup approval; 4 stashes preserved; story counter 00415.

Test accounts

  • test_account_claude.md — Test account — claude@mmffdev.com: claude@mmffdev.com / password, role=user, fixture subscription 00000000-...-0001; created 2026-05-02 for testing.
  • test_accounts_claude_tier.md — Test accounts — claude_N_test@mmffdev.com tier: claude_1_test (user), claude_2_test (padmin), claude_3_test (gadmin), all password123!; never change passwords or emails.

Hard rules & feedback

  • feedback_passwords.md — HARD RULE — never modify user account passwords: never touch password_hash on gadmin@/padmin@/cookra@ etc.; use test account or look up creds in <accounts>.
  • feedback_dev_only.md — HARD RULE — backend is PINNED to dev: env is permanently dev; if marker says staging/prod, silently revert (don't ask); 99% of “lockouts” are env-mismatch.
  • feedback_safety_first.md — Always recommend the safest, best approach: on risky/multi-path work, lead with a ranked safest-first recommendation; never neutral A/B/C menus.
  • feedback_push_often.md — Push migrations + commits often, don't stack: push migrations as they land, not in batches; backfill schema_migrations immediately if applied via raw psql; no gaps in numbering.

CSS conventions

  • css_buttons.md — CSS — canonical .btn rule: every <button> MUST use .btn + variant; bespoke selectors override only positioning/colour, never restate baseline declarations.
  • css_tables.md — CSS — tables use tree_accordion-dense__scroll: .table* family deprecated 2026-05-05; new tables compose from tree_accordion-dense__*; touched files migrate as part of the change; bulk strip lands once all call-sites are converted.

Agent routing

  • feedback_research_paper_writer_model.md — Research-paper writer = Sonnet, investigators = Opus: for <addpaper> / /research --page workflows, route writer agent to Sonnet, keep scan/audit agents on Opus.

Project-state pickups

  • project_archive_map_breadcrumbs.md — Archive map flyout — live breadcrumb rows: ArchiveMapFlyout renders muted “live” intermediates between anchor and archived twigs; new callers MUST pass liveAncestorsMap or flyout breaks (PLA-0006 fix, 2026-05-04).
  • project_launcher_stale_binary.md — Launcher backend stale-binary trap: after a Go source edit /tmp/vector-backend updates on disk but the running launcher process still serves OLD code; SIGKILL to force respawn (SIGTERM ignored).
  • project_portfolio_layers_independent.md — Portfolio layers are tenant-built & independent (max 10): each portfolio_item_types row is one layer with its own flow; tenants pick depth (2 to 10, cap is hard); never assume fixed depth.
  • project_flow_state_construction.md — Flow-state construction across <Table> artefacts (PLA-0015 → PLA-0017): pickup record — Flows-tab UUID bug → centralised hyphen fix in Table.tsx → spec-flag model for tree/accordion/dnd/section/dense. (Companion document: see O001.)

Why a snapshot

The live MEMORY.md is mutable — entries get added, edited, or pruned across sessions. By freezing a copy here in dev/operations/, the project carries a self-contained record of the operational shape of the auto-memory at a known point in time. If the live index ever drifts (e.g. a memory file gets removed and the index entry stays, or vice-versa), this snapshot is the audit anchor.

To regenerate this snapshot at any time, read ~/.claude/projects/-Users-rick-Documents-MMFFDev-Projects-MMFFDev---Vector/memory/MEMORY.md and reformat each bullet into the appropriate section above.
